description: Lock Papyrus & Parchment Writing Surfaces to plant-material papyrus preparation plus prepared animal-skin parchment/vellum, without claiming papyrus domestication or tanned leather working.

checks.reason: Papyrus sheet manufacture requires identifying, harvesting, and processing papyrus stems, but the inspected source does not require domesticated papyrus.
Parchment and vellum use calf, goat, or sheep skins, so managed livestock is an enabling material path for that branch while not being required for papyrus.
Parchment/vellum production depends on cleaning, stretching, scraping, and finishing animal skins for writing.
Papyrus-sheet evidence supports papyrus plant processing, not a direct domestication requirement.
Parchment and vellum are prepared writing skins, so the direct prerequisite is hide preparation rather than tanned leather working.
